sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game has reset. Let me try the simplest hypothesis: paint all 4 white blocks to green.
# First verify initial state
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Initial grid:")
for r in [50, 55, 60]:
    print(f"  {r}: {ascii_lines[r][24:37]}")

# Set paint to green by clicking top purple dot
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 15}])

# Paint all 4 white blocks to green
# Top-center: (50, 30)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30}])
# Mid-left: (55, 25)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25}])
# Mid-right: (55, 35)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35}])
# Bot-center: (60, 30)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30}])

# Check result
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nAfter painting all white blocks green:")
for r in [50, 55, 60]:
    print(f"  {r}: {ascii_lines[r][24:37]}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
